src_text: Because they are everywhere, for everyone. According to researchers at University College London, the UK injectables market will be worth £11.7bn by next year, with Botox and fillers available anywhere from Harley Street to high-street hairdressers. Writing in Grazia last week, the Guardian beauty columnist Sali Hughes described how "women in teaching, policing and the civil service" ask her advice on where to get "good injections." In the US, Botox use by 20- to 29-year-olds has increased 28% since 2010, with gen Z buying into "prejuvenation" (another awful portmanteau), fuelled by the poreless perfection offered by filters, staring at themselves on pandemic screens and social media skinfluencers (argh).
mt_text: 因为随处可见，人人皆可使用。根据伦敦大学学院的研究人员称，英国肉毒杆菌注射市场预计到明年将达到117亿英镑，肉毒杆菌和填充剂产品在哈雷街到普通理发店等各种场所均有销售。上周在《Grazia》杂志上，卫报美容专栏作家萨莉·休斯描述了“教师、警察和公务员”向她咨询“美容注射”的合适地点。在美国，自2010年以来，20至29岁人群的肉毒杆菌注射使用量增加了28%，Z世代受到“提前抗衰老”（另一个难听的组合词）的影响，这得益于滤镜提供的无毛孔完美肌肤，以及在屏幕和社交媒体上观察自己，以及美妆网红的影响。
main_issue: The translation uses a redundant grammatical structure ('根据...称') and a clunky, repetitive list structure at the end that misses cultural nuances like 'pandemic screens'.

src_text: I've always thought that one of the most insoluble aesthetic problems going is remaking a movie masterpiece. I certainly understand the impulse to passionately re-engage such a work, but if the definition of a masterwork is something peerless at what it sought to accomplish, how do you remake it without simply reiterating it? There's a reason no one has tried second versions of Fellini's "8½" or Coppola's "The Godfather" or Polanski's "Chinatown." In the case of Fellini's achievement, is someone going to produce a more harrowing portrait of the self-deluding toxicity of male narcissism? In the case of Coppola's, of the corrosive effects of power? Or in the case of Polanski's, of the Hey-nothing-personal malevolence of late-model capitalism? (Water itself in that movie turns out to be the commodity that's manipulated for profit.)
mt_text: Я всегда считал, что одна из самых сложных эстетических проблем — это переснять кинематографический шедевр. Конечно, я понимаю стремление заново осмыслить такое произведение, но если шедевр по определению — это нечто непревзойдённое в своём замысле, как можно его переснять, не повторяя? Неслучайно никто не брался за вторые версии «8½» Феллини, «Крёстного отца» Копполы или «Китайского квартала» Полански. Разве кто-то сможет превзойти Феллини в изображении самообмана и токсичности мужского нарциссизма? Или Копполу — в показе разъедающей силы власти? Или Полански — в демонстрации безликой жестокости позднего капитализма? (В его фильме даже вода становится товаром, которым манипулируют ради прибыли.)
main_issue: The translation smooths over the specific cynical tone of the 'Hey-nothing-personal' idiom and omits several punchy adjectives like 'harrowing'.

src_text:
A brief note on Play Station controllers to round out the 1st party lineup: I never liked them.
Dual Shock 1-3 felt exactly the same to me with exactly the same flaws: Oversized thumbsticks placed too close together, mushy face/shoulder/trigger buttons that seemed to wear out very quickly, and a D-pad that just felt wrong. Dual Shock 4 fixed a lot of problems, but introduced a bunch of new ones. I got a Dual Sense because some friends had a hard time with asymmetrical thumbsticks, and for some reason local stores were selling them for $20 less than DS4 controllers.
I don't really use this controller: Face buttons are spaced too far apart, my audio in/output keeps defaulting to the controller when it's connected, the bumpers are weirdly tall, and I keep accidentally pressing the track pad when trying to hit menu/share.
They are fine guest controllers that have come a long way in terms of design, but for me they're a novelty at best.
mt_text:
ملاحظة سريعة عن وحدات تحكم بلاي ستيشن لإكمال قائمة المنتجات الأصلية: أنا لم أحبها أبدًا.
شعرت أن Dual Shock 1-3 كانت متشابهة تمامًا، مع نفس العيوب: عصا التحكم كبيرة جدًا ومقربة جدًا من بعضها البعض، وأزرار الوجه/الكتف/الزناد كانت لينة جدًا وكأنها تتآكل بسرعة، ولوحة الاتجاهات كانت تشعر بأنها غير صحيحة. Dual Shock 4 حلت الكثير من المشاكل، لكنها قدمت مجموعة جديدة من المشاكل. اشتريت Dual Sense لأن بعض أصدقائي كانوا يواجهون صعوبة مع عصا التحكم غير المتماثلة، ولسبب ما كانت المتاجر المحلية تبيعها بسعر أقل بـ 20 دولارًا من وحدات تحكم DS4.
أنا لا أستخدم هذه الوحدة كثيرًا: الأزرار متباعدة جدًا، مدخل/مخرج الصوت الخاص بي يعود دائمًا إلى الوحدة عند توصيلها، الأزرار الجانبية مرتفعة بشكل غريب، وأنا أضغط عن طريق الخطأ على لوحة التتبع عندما أحاول الضغط على قائمة/مشاركة.
إنها وحدات تحكم ضيوف جيدة تطورت بشكل كبير من حيث التصميم، لكن بالنسبة لي هي مجرد شيء جديد ومثير للاهتمام في أفضل الأحوال.
main_issue: The translation uses Modern Standard Arabic instead of the requested Egyptian Arabic (ar_EG) and contains a literal personification calque for 'felt wrong'.

src_text:
Declan hesitated only for a second before sliding into his seat next to Ryan, who was already digging in like it was a competitive sport. He glanced at his own portion and, after a quick taste, instantly understood why. The chicken parm was insanely good.
Declan took another bite, savoring the perfect balance of crispy breading and rich marinara. The flavors were way better than he expected, so much so that he couldn't help but mutter, “This is fucking good.”
The second the words left his mouth, the room fell dead silent. Ryan’s fork froze halfway to his mouth, his eyes snapping up to Declan with a mixture of shock and disbelief, like Declan had just unleashed a bomb at the dinner table.
mt_text:
تردد ديكلان لثانية واحدة فقط قبل أن يجلس بجانب رايان، الذي كان يأكل بسرعة وكأنه في منافسة رياضية. ألقى ديكلان نظرة على حصته، وبعد تذوق سريع، فهم على الفور سبب سرعة رايان في الأكل… كان الدجاج المطهو بالبارميزان لذيذًا بشكل خرافي.  
أخذ ديكلان لقمة أخرى، واستمتع بالتوازن المثالي بين القشرة المقرمشة وصلصة المارينارا الغنية. كان الطعم أفضل بكثير مما كان يتوقع، لدرجة أنه لم يستطع إلا أن يقول: “لذيذ للغاية.”  
في اللحظة التي خرجت فيها الكلمات من فمه، ساد الصمت المطبق في الغرفة… تجمدت شوكة رايان في منتصف الطريق إلى فمه، ورفع عينيه نحو ديكلان بمزيج من الصدمة وعدم التصديق، كأن ديكلان قد فجر قنبلة على طاولة العشاء.
main_issue: The translation sanitizes the profanity into a polite phrase, making the characters' subsequent shock and the 'bomb' metaphor completely nonsensical.

src_text:
Kyle cut in, finishing Luc’s thought. “You want us to be his billet family.”
Luc nodded thoughtfully. “I’ve seen firsthand how you and Nicole raised Ryan, and just look at how incredible he turned out. If Declan has a support system like yours, it could be a game changer for him.”
Kyle took a moment to ponder. This wasn’t merely a simple favor; this was a responsibility for another soul. He hadn’t anticipated considering taking in a kid when he read Luc’s text that morning. Still, he would do anything for Luc, and the earnest look in his eyes made it clear just how serious this was to him. That meant it was now serious to Kyle as well.
mt_text:
“你们是想让我们当他的寄养家庭，”凯尔插话说道，补充了卢克的话。
卢克若有所思地点点头。“我亲眼见过你们是怎么教育瑞恩的，而且看看他现在有多么优秀。如果迪克兰也能得到像你们这样的支持，那对他来说将会是巨大的改变。”
凯尔沉思片刻。这不仅仅是一件小事，而是要承担起照顾另一个人的责任。他早上收到卢克的信息时，根本没想过要收养一个孩子。但为了卢克，他愿意做任何事，而且卢克眼中那种真挚的表情也让他明白这件事对卢克来说有多么重要。既然如此，这件事对凯尔来说也变得非常重要了。
main_issue: The translation mistranslates 'taking in a kid' (in a billeting context) as legal adoption (收养), which fundamentally changes the nature of the request.

src_text:
🧵 I now have a German telefonnummer! Exciting!
That travel sim I bought did help bridge the gap, but the service was horrendous and relied on roaming. I was also under the impression it could dial out, which it could not. I already needed to call a German number.
The SIM salesperson was also an expat (not american) and I had the nicest conversation! He wanted tips with learning programming*, and I asked how long to learn German and he was super inspiring! Saying that those who must learn—like for work—learn much more and faster! He encouraged me to keep with it and in 3 months I should be fine 🫶. 
I will try to keep my numver when I inevitably switch. I chose this from convenience. Being that he didnt even require me an address, only an ID to activate. 
Aside: no, I didnt think D-Netz was a Deez Nuts reference... Ofc not xD. You're silly for even thinking that haha
(*hes starting in Python! Which I think is the MOST fun, even if someone has to unlearn bad habits. I think its a valuable litmus test for those to see if programming is for them)
mt_text:
🧵 Teď mám německé telefonnummer! Vzrušující!
Ta cestovní SIM karta, kterou jsem si koupila, pomohla překlenout mezeru, ale služba byla hrozná a spoléhala na roaming. Také jsem měla dojem, že může volat ven, což nemohla. Už jsem potřebovala zavolat na německé číslo.
Prodavač SIM karet byl také expat (ne Američan) a měli jsme nejmilejší rozhovor! Chtěl tipy na učení programování* a já jsem se zeptala, jak dlouho se učí německy, a on byl super inspirativní! Říkal, že ti, kteří se musí učit – třeba kvůli práci – se učí mnohem víc a rychleji! Povzbudil mě, abych v tom pokračovala, a za 3 měsíce bych měla být v pořádku 🫶.
Budu se snažit si své číslo ponechat, až ho nevyhnutelně přepnu. Vybrala jsem si to z pohodlnosti. Protože ani nežádal adresu, jenom ID k aktivaci.
Mimochodem: ne, nemyslela jsem si, že D-Netz je odkaz na Deez Nuts... Samozřejmě ne xD. Jste hloupí, že si to vůbec myslíte haha
(*začíná v Pythonu! Což si myslím, že je NEJVĚTŠÍ zábava, i když se někdo musí odnaučit špatné návyky. Myslím, že je to cenný litmusový test pro ty, kteří chtějí zjistit, zda je programování pro ně)
main_issue: The translation relies heavily on literal calques of English idioms and fails to adapt the tone to natural, informal Czech.

src_text:
X: Yeah, I'm looking for an entertaining tourist attraction, can point me in the direction of some places to check out?
Y: I have about 5 different entertainment venues if that is what you are looking for. Do you have a preference on the area its located in?
X: No preference, please just pick one and give me the postcode and address.
Y: I think you'd enjoy the Funky Fun House. They're located at 8 mercers row, mercers row industrial estate. Their postcode is cb58hy. Can I help you with anything else?
X: What area is the Funky Fun House located?
Y: Funky fun house is located in the east area. Can I help you with anything else today?
X: I am also looking for a restaurant that's moderately priced and serves british food.
Y: I have five different restaurants to choose from. There are 4 in the centre of town, and 1 in the west. Do you have a preference?
X: Let's try making at booking at the one in the west.

src_text:
Declan hesitated only for a second before sliding into his seat next to Ryan, who was already digging in like it was a competitive sport. He glanced at his own portion and, after a quick taste, instantly understood why. The chicken parm was insanely good.
Declan took another bite, savoring the perfect balance of crispy breading and rich marinara. The flavors were way better than he expected, so much so that he couldn't help but mutter, “This is fucking good.”
The second the words left his mouth, the room fell dead silent. Ryan’s fork froze halfway to his mouth, his eyes snapping up to Declan with a mixture of shock and disbelief, like Declan had just unleashed a bomb at the dinner table.

src_text: Overexposure, of course, is the death knell for cool in most cases (or at least self-conscious, trend-following "coolness," as I mean it here). This is particularly true amid the one-upmanship of contemporary food and drink culture which, as defined by social media, is always about being the first to find the undiscovered or most novel thing - the "hidden gem," the bakery and "pasta space" in London's zone 6 or what have you ("Come with me to the pub in Richmond that regularly floods"). And while Guinness's current round of popularity began because it was once viewed among younger pubgoers as a left-field choice - an "if you know, you know" order - it now couldn't be less of a secret.

src_text:
A brief note on Play Station controllers to round out the 1st party lineup: I never liked them.
Dual Shock 1-3 felt exactly the same to me with exactly the same flaws: Oversized thumbsticks placed too close together, mushy face/shoulder/trigger buttons that seemed to wear out very quickly, and a D-pad that just felt wrong. Dual Shock 4 fixed a lot of problems, but introduced a bunch of new ones. I got a Dual Sense because some friends had a hard time with asymmetrical thumbsticks, and for some reason local stores were selling them for $20 less than DS4 controllers.
I don't really use this controller: Face buttons are spaced too far apart, my audio in/output keeps defaulting to the controller when it's connected, the bumpers are weirdly tall, and I keep accidentally pressing the track pad when trying to hit menu/share.
They are fine guest controllers that have come a long way in terms of design, but for me they're a novelty at best.

كان Dual Shock 1-3 يبدو لي متطابقًا تمامًا مع نفس العيوب: عصي التحكم كبيرة الحجم موضوعة قريبة جدًا من بعضها البعض، وأزرار الوجه/الكتف/الزناد اللينة التي تبدو وكأنها تتلف بسرعة كبيرة، ولوحة اتجاهات تبدو خاطئة تمامًا. لقد أصلح Dual Shock 4 العديد من المشكلات، لكنه أضاف مجموعة جديدة منها. لقد اشتريت Dual Sense لأن بعض الأصدقاء واجهوا صعوبة مع عصي التحكم غير المتماثلة، ولسبب ما كانت المتاجر المحلية تبيعها بسعر أقل بـ 20 دولارًا من أجهزة تحكم DS4.

src_text:
Declan shifted in his seat, still trying to wrap his head around it. Living with Kyle Barnes. Having breakfast across the table from the guy who used to light it up in the league. It was a lot to take in.
Kyle let out a soft chuckle, picking up on the hesitation in Declan’s eyes. “It’s a lot to process, huh?”
Declan didn’t answer, but the look on his face said enough. He watched Kyle carefully, the weight of it all pressing in.
Kyle leaned back in his chair, settling into a more relaxed posture, but his eyes stayed sharp and knowing. “I’ve been where you’re sitting. New town. New team. Big pressure. It can mess with your head if you let it.”

لم يجب ديكلان، لكن نظرة وجهه قالت الكثير. كان يراقب كايل بعناية، حيث كان ثقل كل ذلك يضغط عليه.

src_text:
Declan’s mind flickered through every worry he’d been carrying since stepping off that bus. Was Kyle reading him that easily? It felt like he was peeling back layers without even trying. That low, familiar buzz of uncertainty crept up again, and somehow, Kyle seemed to catch it without a word.
“I know it’s not easy, kid.” Kyle gave a small, almost fatherly sigh. His voice was lower now; gentle, but steady. “But here’s the thing. If you stay with us, you’re not just crashing at a billet house. I’m offering you space to breathe. A place to land when everything else feels like it’s spinning.”

src_text: F. W. Murnau's "Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror" is itself a remake - an unauthorized adaptation of "Dracula," Bram Stoker's compulsively readable fever dream of a novel - and Murnau and the screenwriter Henrik Galeen retained much of what was arresting about the original while slipping in their own major changes: They're responsible, for example, for the now-set-in-stone tradition that sunlight can destroy the vampire, a notion nowhere in Stoker's book. I likely saw "Nosferatu" at too impressionable an age. I was 6, PBS was showing such things and my babysitter was simply glad I wasn't burning down the house. But I would have been flattened by it whenever I saw it. It was like having felt a draft from a grave. (Its effects were so long-lasting that 30-something years later I published a novel inspired by the film and its production.) The whole thing wasn't so much petrifying as insidiously unsettling, and all of that started with the figure of Nosferatu himself. Max Schreck's performance is, 102 years later, still the benchmark for sinister and dignified repulsiveness. Schreck's vampire has the stillness of a figure in a bad dream or a spider on its web, and the world he inhabits is at times equally disconcerting. After our hero Hutter's first frightening night in Nosferatu's castle, he notices in the mirror that something has bitten his neck, and he smiles.
mt_text: Snímek „Nosferatu, symfonie hrůzy“ od F. W. Murnaua je sám o sobě remakem – neautorizovanou adaptací „Drákuly“, horečnatého snového románu Brama Stokera, který se čte jedním dechem – a Murnau se scenáristou Henrikem Galeenem si ponechali mnohé z toho, co bylo na originálu strhující, a zároveň do něj vložili své vlastní zásadní změny: Jsou zodpovědní například za dnes již zavedenou tradici, že sluneční světlo dokáže upíra zničit, což je myšlenka, která se ve Stokerově knize nikde neobjevuje. „Nosferatu“ jsem pravděpodobně viděl v příliš útlém věku. Bylo mi šest let, veřejnoprávní televize tehdy takové věci vysílala a moje chůva byla prostě ráda, že jsem nepodpálil dům. Ale srazilo by mě to na kolena, ať už bych to viděl kdykoli. Bylo to, jako bych ucítil závan z hrobu. (Jeho účinky byly tak dlouhodobé, že jsem o zhruba třicet let později vydal román inspirovaný tímto filmem a jeho natáčením.) Celé to nebylo ani tak děsivé, jako spíše zákeřně znepokojivé, a všechno to začalo postavou samotného Nosferatu. Výkon Maxe Schrecka je i po 102 letech stále etalonem zlověstné a důstojné odpornosti. Schreckův upír má nehybnost postavy ze zlého snu nebo pavouka na pavučině a svět, který obývá, je občas stejně znepokojivý. Poté, co náš hrdina Hutter prožije první děsivou noc na Nosferatově hradě, všimne si v zrcadle, že ho něco kouslo do krku, a usměje se.
main_issue: The translation is highly professional and natural, with only very minor stylistic choices or morphological preferences being debatable.

src_text:
“Where are the parents?” Kyle asked, the question feeling all too obvious given his own experiences. 
He and Nicole had made it a priority to effectively manage their son’s off-ice behavior throughout his upbringing, committed to ensuring no negative bullet points marred his hockey resume. Any acting out was always nipped in the bud, immediately.
“Mom’s not in the picture; hasn’t been since he was a baby, from what I hear,” Luc replied. “And the dad isn’t very present; he doesn’t show up to any games. Just writes the checks when he needs to.”
“Shit,” was all Kyle could manage in response.
mt_text:
“Де батьки?” – запитав Кайла, і питання здавалося надто очевидним, враховуючи його власний досвід.
Він і Ніколь робили все можливе, щоб ефективно контролювати поведінку їхнього сина поза льодом, прагнучи забезпечити, щоб у його хокейному резюме не було жодних негативних пунктів. Будь-яке непослушне витівки негайно припиняли.
“Матері немає в його житті; не було з тих пір, як він зовсім малий, – відповів Люк. – А батько майже не проявляє інтересу; він не з'являється на жодних іграх. Лише виписує чеки, коли потрібно.”
“Блін,” – все, що Кайла зміг вимовити у відповідь.

Declan didn’t even realize he was nodding until the motion was halfway done.
Something about the way Kyle spoke; calm, direct, like he actually got it. It cut through the usual static in Declan’s brain. He didn’t know what was happening, not really. No adult had ever made him feel that before. Not a teacher. Not a coach. Not even his own dad.

كان هناك شيء في طريقة كايل التي تحدث بها؛ هادئة، مباشرة، وكأنها فهمت الأمر بالفعل. لقد اخترقت الصخب المعتاد في دماغ ديكلان. لم يعرف ما الذي يحدث، ليس حقًا. فقط أنه، ولسبب ما، كان هذا الرجل لديه بالفعل نوعًا من السيطرة عليه. ليس بطريقة قسرية. فقط... ثابتة. صلبة. مثل شخص ما يعني ما يقوله ولن يتخلى عنك عندما تصبح الأمور صعبة.

src_text: Music to my ears, really - I'm just pleased that while years ago you could walk into a pub and reasonably expect that Guinness wouldn't be served there, these days I can pretty much get my preferred pint anywhere. And, among all of the arguments to explain the surge in approval for the drink, it is important to acknowledge the obvious one: its smooth texture and subtly rich taste do just combine to offer something genuinely delicious, even when you get a mediocre one.

src_text: It's not a Nordic culinary microtrend or a sex thing, but a beauty treatment, in which "Polydeoxyribonucleotides (PDRN) derived from purified fragments of DNA extracted from yes, "salmon sperm"" are injected into your face. It does seem to work: proper research has found PDRN has wound-repairing properties in hard-to-treat contexts such as diabetic ulcers and deep burns. Good news (unless you're a male salmon). But what is it doing in healthy faces?
mt_text: これは北欧の食文化における一時的な流行でもなければ、性的な意味でもない。これは美容治療の一環で、「精製したDNA断片から抽出したポリデオキシリボヌクレオチド（PDRN）――そう、『サケの精子』由来の成分――」を顔に直接注入するものだ。実際に効果があるようで、適切な研究によれば、PDRNには糖尿病性潰瘍や重度の火傷など、治癒が困難な創傷部位の修復作用があることが確認されている。男性サケを除いては朗報だろう。しかし、健康な人間の顔にこれを使用する目的は何なのか？
main_issue: The translation uses '男性' (human male) for a salmon instead of the biologically appropriate 'オス' (male animal).

src_text:
Declan hesitated only for a second before sliding into his seat next to Ryan, who was already digging in like it was a competitive sport. He glanced at his own portion and, after a quick taste, instantly understood why. The chicken parm was insanely good.
Declan took another bite, savoring the perfect balance of crispy breading and rich marinara. The flavors were way better than he expected, so much so that he couldn't help but mutter, “This is fucking good.”
The second the words left his mouth, the room fell dead silent. Ryan’s fork froze halfway to his mouth, his eyes snapping up to Declan with a mixture of shock and disbelief, like Declan had just unleashed a bomb at the dinner table.
mt_text:
डेक्लेन कुछ पल खातिर हिचकिचाइल, फेरू आपन सीट पर रायन के बगल में बइठ गइल, जे पहिले से ही ई अंदाज में काम कर रहल रहलन जइसे कि ई एगो प्रतिस्पर्धात्मक खेल होखे। ऊ अपना हिस्सा के देखल आ, जल्दी से स्वाद लेके, तुरंते समझ गइल काहे खातिर। चिकन परम लाजवाब रहल।
डेक्लान एक दोसरा कौर खा लिहलस, सुन्दर संतुलन में पॉचरी ब्रेडिंग आ समृद्ध मारिनारा के आनंद लेत रहलस। स्वाद ओकरा उम्मीद से कहीं बेहतर रहल, अइसन बढ़िया कि ऊ खामोश ना रह सकल, आ कहे लागल, "ई त बहरी जबरदस्त बा।"
जइसे ही ऊ सब शब्द उनकर मुँह से निकलल, कमरा अचानक से बिलकुल चुप हो गइल। रयान के कांटा आधा रास्ता में रुक गइल, ओकर आँखें डक्लन की ओर चटकन से बढ़ल, चौंकावट आ अविश्वास के साथ, जइसे डक्लन अभी-अभी खाना के टेबल पर बम फेंकल होखे।
main_issue: The translation mistreats 'Parm' (Parmesan) as the Sanskrit/Hindi word 'Param' (supreme) and uses literal, unidiomatic phrasing for English idioms like 'digging in' and 'snapping up'.

mt_text:
عقل ديكلان كان يتأرجح بين كل القلقات اللي كان بيحملها من لحظة ما نزل من الباص. هل كان كايل بيقرأه بسهولة كده؟ كان الموضوع كأنه بيقشر طبقات من شخصيته من غير حتى ما يحاول. هذا الهمس الخفيف المألوف من الشك بدأ يزحف مرة تانية، وكأن كايل فهمه من غير ما يتكلم كلمة واحدة.
“أنا عارف إن الموضوع مش سهل، يا ولد.” كايل زفر زفرة صغيرة، كأنها زفرة أب. صوته أصبح أهدأ الآن؛ لطيف، لكن ثابت. “لكن هنا بقى الموضوع. لو فكرت تبقى معانا، أنت مش بس قاعد في مكان مؤقت. أنا بعرض عليك مساحة تاخد فيها نفس. مكان ترتاح فيه لما كل حاجة تانية تحس إنها بتلف.”
main_issue: The translation suffers from a jarring mix of Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) and Egyptian colloquial, alongside literal translations of English idioms.

src_text: Some set designs instantly make sense. Paul Wills's clever arrangement of television screens and speakers, all linked up by glowing capillary-like tubes, encapsulates Malorie Blackman's 1997 novel about a boy whose pig-heart transplant leads to a media frenzy. Andrew Exeter's lighting switches from blood red to cool blue for scenes when 13-year-old Cameron repeatedly puts himself - and, eventually, his new heart - to the test when diving at his local pool.
mt_text: Деякі сценічні декорації одразу стають зрозумілими. Винахідливе розташування телевізійних екранів і динаміків, з’єднаних світяться трубками, схожими на капіляри, від Пола Вілса, відображає роман Малорі Блекмен 1997 року про хлопчика, чия трансплантація свинячого серця спричинила медійну істерію. У сценах, коли 13-річний Камерон неодноразово випробовує себе – а згодом і своє нове серце – під час плавання у місцевому басейні, освітлення Ендрю Ексетера змінюється з кроваво-червоного на прохолодно-синє.
main_issue: The translation contains a significant grammatical error in a participle construction and a lexical error (Russicism/misspelling) in 'кроваво-червоного'.

src_text: I shouldn't be surprised - actual children are buying expensive anti-ageing potions and fretting about their nonexistent crow's feet now. But still, this dramatic normalisation of invasive beauty procedures is unnerving.
mt_text: لا ينبغي أن أكون مندهشاً - فالأطفال الحقيقيون يشترون الآن جرعات مكلفة لمكافحة الشيخوخة ويشعرون بالقلق إزاء تجاعيد العين غير الموجودة. ولكن، لا يزال هذا التطبيع الدرامي للإجراءات التجميلية الغازية أمراً مزعجاً.
main_issue: The translation suffers from heavy literalism, using calques like 'الغازية' for invasive and 'الدرامي' for dramatic, which sound unnatural in Arabic.

src_text:
Declan shook his hand, barely keeping the awe off his face. A breath slipped out, half disbelief, half nerves, and before he could stop himself, his mouth got ahead of his brain.
“I had you on my fantasy team last year. Not gonna lie, you lost me the championship.”
Declan pressed his lips together the second the words left his mouth, the grin fading as panic crept in. Nice one, he thought. Way to chirp an NHL vet five minutes into meeting him. He resisted the urge to bury his face in his hands.
A snicker sounded behind him. Declan glanced over his shoulder to see Coach Luc with a hand half-covering his mouth, doing a poor job of hiding his amusement.
mt_text:
صافحه ديكلان، وهو يكاد يخفي الدهشة عن وجهه. انزلقت أنفاسه، نصفها عدم تصديق ونصفها توتر، وقبل أن يتمكن من إيقاف نفسه، سبق فمه عقله.
“لقد كنت ضمن فريقي الخيالي في العام الماضي. لن أكذب، لقد خسرتني البطولة.”
ضغط ديكلان على شفتيه بمجرد أن خرجت الكلمات من فمه، واختفت الابتسامة مع تسلل الذعر. حركة جيدة، فكر. طريقة رائعة للسخرية من محترف في دوري الهوكي الوطني بعد خمس دقائق من لقائه. قاوم الرغبة في دفن وجهه في يديه.
صدرت ضحكة خافتة من خلفه. نظر ديكلان فوق كتفه ليرى المدرب لوك يغطي فمه بيديه جزئيًا، وهو يحاول جاهدًا إخفاء مرحه.
main_issue: The translation is a literal, word-for-word mapping of English idioms into Modern Standard Arabic, failing to use the requested Egyptian dialect or natural Arabic phrasing.

src_text: "Talk of badges reminds of the famous one from the 1960s-70s," says Merran Loewenthal of Birchgrove. ""It Begins When You Sink in His Arms and Ends with Your Arms in His Sink."" Jonty Grinter of Katoomba has one that reads "Chronically Advantaged" and adds that "it should be standard issue for all over seventy."
mt_text: "الحديث عن الأوسمة يذكرني بالوسام الشهير من الستينيات والسبعينيات"، تقول ميران لوينثال من بيرشغروف. ""يبدأ عندما تغرقين في ذراعيه وينتهي بذراعيك في حوضه."" جونتي غرينتر من كاتومبا لديه وسام يقول "محظوظ مزمنًا" ويضيف أنه "يجب أن يكون إصدارًا قياسيًا لكل من هم فوق السبعين."
main_issue: The translation uses stiff, literal calques for idiomatic expressions and misses the casual register of 'badges' (using 'medals' instead).

src_text: For those wondering, I still have my hotel room. I played it safe and didn't want to rely on being able to get keys yesterday with luggage in tow
mt_text: Для тех, кто спрашивал, номер в отеле у меня всё ещё есть. Я просто перестраховался и не хотел полагаться на то, что смогу получить ключи вчера с багажом за плечами.
main_issue: The idiom 'за плечами' refers to carrying a backpack or metaphorical life experience, which doesn't fit 'luggage in tow' if it involves suitcases.

src_text:
🧵 I am not officially registered in Germany! Anmeldung in-hand! Woo 🎉
And I knew a lot for German than I thought in this exchange! I practiced German and she practiced English. (we both were being humble, but her English was good) 
"Im sorry to ask... But are you man, woman, or divers?" 💜💜
We proceeded to talk about that. And she said she wondered if maybe thats why I come from states and I was like: yes, its so nice to hear other people understand. Omg.
She said shes happy that people [like me] come and see Germany as a safe place 🫶
mt_text:
🧵 Я офіційно не зареєстрована в Німеччині! Але документ про реєстрацію вже є! Ура 🎉
І я зрозумів, що знаю про німецьку мову значно більше, ніж думав, завдяки цій розмові! Я практикував німецьку, а вона – англійську (ми обидва були скромними, але її англійська була хороша).
Вибачте, що питаю... Але чи ви чоловік, жінка чи трансгендерна особа? 💜💜
Ми стали обговорювати це. І вона сказала, що дивується, чи, можливо, саме тому я з Штатів, і я відповіла: так, так приємно чути, коли інші люди розуміють. Боже мій.
Вона сказала, що рада, що люди [такі, як я] приїжджають і бачать Німеччину як безпечне місце 🫶
main_issue: The translation suffers from jarring gender inconsistency (switching between masculine and feminine for the same speaker) and a stiff register that misses the casual tone of the source.

mt_text:
Деклан му је чврсто пружио руку, покушавајући да сакрије своје одушевљење. Из њега је излетела уздахнула реч, мешавина неверице и нервозе, и пре него што је успео да се заустави, његове речи су „победиле“ његову памет.
„Имали сте вас у мојој фантази лиги прошле године. Морам да признам, изгубили сте ми шампионат.“
Деклан је одмах стегнуо усне чим је то изговорио, а осмех му је нестао док га је обузимала паника. „Одлично“, помислио је. „Сјајан начин да се у првих пет минута упознавања са искусним играчем из НХЛ-а похвалиш.“ Покушао је да одолети жељи да сакрије лице у рукама.
Чуо се тих смех иза њега. Деклан је погледао преко рамена и видео тренера Лука, који је руком покушавао да сакрије осмех, али безуспешно.
main_issue: The translation contains severe grammatical errors (e.g., 'Имали сте вас', 'да одолети') and fails to use standard Serbian idioms for common English expressions.

src_text: Anyway, we know how this ends. In the largest sense, with the cancellation of programmes mandated democratically in Congress by a bunch of unelected goons in puffer vests. And in the smallest sense, with one of these 22-year-old jerks spilling his Big Gulp cup of Mountain Dew over a keyboard at the Treasury and wiping the social security data of 70 million Americans. I look forward to watching as Big Balls and co find new ways to tank an economy even more efficiently and irreversibly than Brexit.
mt_text: खैर, हमनी के पता बा कि एकर अंत कइसे होई। सबसे बड़ अर्थ में, कांग्रेस में लोकतांत्रिक तरीका से अनिवार्य कइल गइल कार्यक्रम के पफर वेस्ट पहिनले कुछ बिना चुनल गुंडा लोग द्वारा रद्द कइला से। आ सबसे छोट अर्थ में, एह 22 साल के बेवकूफ में से केहू के ट्रेजरी में कीबोर्ड पर आपन माउंटेन ड्यू के बिग गल्प कप गिरा दिहला से आ 7 करोड़ अमेरिकी लोग के सामाजिक सुरक्षा डेटा के मिटा दिहला से। हम ई देखे खातिर उत्सुक बानी कि कइसे बिग बॉल्स आ उनकर साथी लोग ब्रेक्सिट से भी अधिका कुशलता से आ अपरिवर्तनीय रूप से अर्थव्यवस्था के डुबावे के नया तरीका खोजेला।
main_issue: The translation uses overly formal, Sanskritized vocabulary (tatsam) which clashes with the sarcastic, informal tone of the source's 'goons' and 'jerks' imagery.

Declan shook his hand, barely keeping the awe off his face. A breath slipped out, half disbelief, half nerves, and before he could stop himself, his mouth got ahead of his brain.

A snicker sounded behind him. Declan glanced over his shoulder to see Coach Luc with a hand half-covering his mouth, doing a poor job of hiding his amusement.
mt_text:
Declan je potrpao za ruku, te je s lica teško izbacivao strah. Iz usta mu je iscurila uzdaha, djelomično iz nevjere, a djelomično iz živaca, a prije nego što se mogao suzdržati, usta su mu pobijedila mozak.
„Prošle godine ste bili na mom fantastičnom timu. Neću lagati, izgubili ste me u prvenstvu.”
Declan je stisnuo usne u trenutku kad su mu riječi izašle iz usta, a osmijeh je nestao dok je u njega ušla panika. „Lijep je potez”, pomislio je. „Kako je moguće da se tako smijete NHL veteranu pet minuta nakon što ste ga upoznali.” Oporavio se od nagona da se objesi na lice.
Slijedi se čuo smjeh. Declan je pogledao preko ramena i vidio je trenera Luca s rukom koja je djelomično prekrivala usta, a koji je loše pokušavao sakriti svoj smijeh.
main_issue: The translation is riddled with critical errors, including false friends, literal calques of idioms, and nonsensical phrasing that fails to convey the source meaning.

src_text: I shouldn't be surprised - actual children are buying expensive anti-ageing potions and fretting about their nonexistent crow's feet now. But still, this dramatic normalisation of invasive beauty procedures is unnerving.
mt_text: لا ينبغي أن أتفاجئ - الأطفال الفعليون يشترون الآن مراهم مكلفة مضادة للتقدم في السن ويقلقون بشأن التجاعيد غير الموجودة حول أعينهم. لكن لا يزال هذا التطبيع الدرامي للإجراءات التجميلية الغازية مزعجًا.
main_issue: The translation relies on literal calques for technical and descriptive terms like 'invasive', 'dramatic', and 'actual', resulting in unnatural Arabic.

src_text: The fact there is no story here makes Meghan's insistence that she is getting in front of the story all the more enjoyable, but the more curious aspect of the announcement is the "you" to whom she refers and the idea she clearly has of her audience. The celebrity lifestyle space is crowded, ferocious and notoriously fickle, littered with the carcasses of former brand leaders such as Chrissy Teigen. Target and Bloomingdales distanced themselves from the star around the same time as she was accused of, and later apologised for, cyberbullying a woman called Courtney. The space is also dominated by market leaders such as Gwyneth Paltrow and Rachel Zoe.
mt_text: Той факт, що тут немає історії, робить наполегливість Меган стосовно того, що вона випереджає історію, ще більш приємною, але найцікавішою особливістю оголошення є "ви", на кого вона посилається, і ідея, яку вона чітко має про свою аудиторію. Простір для знаменитостей переповнений, жорстокий і відомий своєю мінливістю, захаращений трупами колишніх бренд-лідерів, таких як Кріссі Тейген. Target і Bloomingdales дистанціювалися від зірки приблизно в той самий час, коли її звинуватили, а потім вибачилися за кібербулінг жінки на ім'я Кортні. Простір також домінують лідери ринку, такі як Гвінет Пелтроу та Рейчел Зое.
main_issue: The translation contains a major subject-verb agreement error regarding who apologized and relies on heavy structural calques from English.

src_text: This will be the year that Guinness loses its cool. Cheers to that!
mt_text: Цього року бренд Guinness почне втрачати вплив. Вітаємо з цим!
main_issue: The translation fails to capture the casual, idiomatic nature of a toast, turning 'Cheers to that' into a stiff, formal congratulation.

src_text: there's a lot of things that are wrong with america. this doesn't clock the 15th percentile. it's just an average sort of excess, another straw on the like last descendant of the great plains buffalo's ailing back. even so, even being as jaded to the way that americans eat as i am, having ordered a one-scoop cup of superman flavor ice cream last night, i find that i have not and could never reach rock bottom. there is a 121st day of sodom, my uncle who works at nintendo told me about it.
mt_text: هناك الكثير من الأشياء الخاطئة في أمريكا. هذا لا يصل إلى نسبة 15% من الأسوأ. إنه مجرد إفراط متوسط، شعرة أخرى على ظهر البقرة المريضة من السهول العظمى. حتى أنني، على الرغم من تشككي في الطريقة التي يأكل بها الأمريكيون، بعد أن طلبت كوباً من نكهة سوبرمان للآيس كريم الليلة الماضية، أجد أنني لم أصل إلى القاع ولم أستطع الوصول إليه أبداً. هناك يوم 121 من السدوم، أخبرني عمي الذي يعمل في نينتندو عن ذلك.
main_issue: The translation is overly literal and fails to capture the cynical, internet-inflected tone, particularly mangling the metaphorical imagery of the buffalo.

src_text:
Declan hesitated only for a second before sliding into his seat next to Ryan, who was already digging in like it was a competitive sport. He glanced at his own portion and, after a quick taste, instantly understood why. The chicken parm was insanely good.
Declan took another bite, savoring the perfect balance of crispy breading and rich marinara. The flavors were way better than he expected, so much so that he couldn't help but mutter, “This is fucking good.”
The second the words left his mouth, the room fell dead silent. Ryan’s fork froze halfway to his mouth, his eyes snapping up to Declan with a mixture of shock and disbelief, like Declan had just unleashed a bomb at the dinner table.
mt_text:
Declan zaváhal jen na vteřinu, než si sedl na své místo vedle Ryana, který už se do jídla pustil, jako by to byl soutěžní sport. Podíval se na svou porci a po rychlé ochutnávce okamžitě pochopil proč. Kuřecí parmezán byl neuvěřitelně dobrý.
Declan si vzal další sousto a vychutnával si dokonalou rovnováhu křupavé strouhankové krustičky a bohaté marinara omáčky. Chutě byly mnohem lepší, než očekával, natolik, že si nemohl pomoct a zamumlal: „To je kurva dobrý."
Ve chvíli, kdy mu ta slova vyšla z úst, v místnosti nastalo hrobové ticho. Ryanova vidlička zamrzla v půli cesty k ústům, oči mu vyletěly k Declanovi se směsí šoku a nevěry, jako by Declan právě odpálil bombu u jídelního stolu.
main_issue: The translation uses 'nevěry' (infidelity/adultery) instead of 'nevěřícnosti' (disbelief), which is a significant semantic error.

src_text: For those wondering, I still have my hotel room. I played it safe and didn't want to rely on being able to get keys yesterday with luggage in tow
mt_text: 对于那些好奇的人，我仍然有我的酒店房间。我采取了安全措施，不想依赖昨天带着行李就能拿到钥匙。
main_issue: The phrase 'played it safe' is mistranslated as taking security measures rather than being cautious, and the overall tone is stiff translationese.

src_text: I suppose we tweakment holdouts will have to wait, and hope, for the tide to turn. As Ozempic and shiny veneers may make thinness and Hollywood teeth look boring and basic, perhaps perfect faces will fall from fashion? I mention the veneers, because when I was watching the Bridget Jones movie, I become fascinated by Chiwetel Ejiofor's teeth. They're absolutely lovely, yes, but sort of ... normal? One lower one peeps out from slightly behind the others. It only added to his charm. Hugh Grant's 64-year-old face looked charismatically crumpled too. Maybe when everyone has been homogenised to a glazed sheen of perfection, sagging, bumps, gaping pores and rough patches will be the height of desirability. At that point, I'll be ready for my closeup.
mt_text:
我想，我们这些坚持不做微整形的人，恐怕只能等待并期盼着潮流的逆转了。当奥兹匹克（Ozempic）和亮白牙贴面让纤瘦身材和好莱坞式皓齿显得乏味又普通时，或许完美的脸蛋也会就此失宠？
我之所以提到牙贴面，是因为在看《BJ单身日记》系列电影时，我完全被切瓦特·埃加福特的牙齿迷住了。是的，他的牙齿非常好看，但又有点……普通？他下排的一颗牙齿从牙列中微微探出，但这反而更增添了他的魅力。休·格兰特那张64岁的脸庞也一样，虽布满褶皱，却充满魅力。
也许当所有人都被同质化，变得完美无瑕、光滑如釉时，皮肤松弛、疙瘩、毛孔粗大和肤质粗糙反而会成为最令人向往的特质。到那时，我就准备好迎接我的特写镜头了。
main_issue: The word '褶皱' (folds/creases) is more commonly used for fabric or paper; for a human face, '皱纹' (wrinkles) is the standard collocation.

src_text: Music to my ears, really - I'm just pleased that while years ago you could walk into a pub and reasonably expect that Guinness wouldn't be served there, these days I can pretty much get my preferred pint anywhere. And, among all of the arguments to explain the surge in approval for the drink, it is important to acknowledge the obvious one: its smooth texture and subtly rich taste do just combine to offer something genuinely delicious, even when you get a mediocre one.
mt_text: موسيقى في ودني، حقيقي - أنا مبسوط بس إن بينما زمان كنت تقدر تدخل حانة وتتوقع بشكل معقول إن الجينيس مش هيتقدم هناك، النهارده أقدر أجيب الكوبايه المفضلة بتاعتي من أي حتة تقريباً. وبين كل الحجج اللي بتفسر الارتفاع في الموافقة على المشروب، مهم نعترف بالواضح: ملمسه الناعم وطعمه الغني الخفيف بيتجمعوا فعلاً عشان يقدموا حاجة لذيذة حقيقي، حتى لما تجيب واحدة متوسطة.
main_issue: The translation relies on literal calques for idioms and collocations, particularly 'music to my ears' and 'surge in approval', which sound unnatural in Egyptian Arabic.

src_text: Here's a quick, fun, instructive story about what not to do, especially if you identify as [looking at Jim Grandone's page] "Political campaign veteran. Only Democrats[.] Public/media relations professional[.] Married and enjoying beach life in the State of Washington[.] Onward to 2026"

src_text:
Declan shifted in his seat, still trying to wrap his head around it. Living with Kyle Barnes. Having breakfast across the table from the guy who used to light it up in the league. It was a lot to take in.
Kyle let out a soft chuckle, picking up on the hesitation in Declan’s eyes. “It’s a lot to process, huh?”
Declan didn’t answer, but the look on his face said enough. He watched Kyle carefully, the weight of it all pressing in.
Kyle leaned back in his chair, settling into a more relaxed posture, but his eyes stayed sharp and knowing. “I’ve been where you’re sitting. New town. New team. Big pressure. It can mess with your head if you let it.”
mt_text:
Деклан се померио у столици, још увек покушавајући да схвати све то. Живот са Кајлом Барнсом. Доручак преко пута човека који је некад блистао у лиги. Било је то много за схватање.
Кајл је тихо закикотао, приметивши оклевање у Декланових очима. „Много је тога за обраду, зар не?"
Деклан није одговорио, али израз на његовом лицу је говорио довољно. Пажљиво је посматрао Кајла, док га је тежина свега притискала.
Кајл се наслонио у столици, заузевши опуштенији став, али очи су му остале оштре и свезнајуће. „Био сам тамо где си ти сада. Нови град. Нови тим. Велики притисак. Може да ти помрси главу ако дозволиш."
main_issue: The translation contains a grammatical case error and relies on literal calques for idioms like 'a lot to process' and 'mess with your head'.

src_text: Post-century, Breetzke punished O'Rourke with two boundaries and a six in a single over, thus accelerating South Africa's late-innings scoring and building momentum for the Proteas. While his partnership with Smith provided early stability, he went on to forge another century stand for the fourth wicket with Wiaan Mulder (64), pushing South Africa past the 300-run mark. Breetzke, who will later join the Lucknow Super Giants in IPL 2025, exhibited complete dominance at the crease, enabling South Africa to establish a formidable total and providing their bowlers with a substantial platform in their bowling defence.
mt_text: Po skončení století se Breetzke pomstil O'Rourkovi dvěma boundary a šesti bodů v jednom overu, čímž urychlil skórování Jihoafrické republiky v pozdějších fázích zápasu a vytvořil tak impuls pro Proteas. Zatímco jeho partnerství se Smithem poskytlo brzkou stabilitu, pokračoval ve vytváření dalšího století pro čtvrtou wicket s Wiaanem Mulderem (64), čímž Jihoafrickou republiku posunul přes hranici 300 bodů. Breetzke, který se později připojí k Lucknow Super Giants v IPL 2025, prokázal úplnou dominanci na pálce, což umožnilo Jihoafrické republice vytvořit impozantní skóre a poskytlo jejich nadhazovačům významnou platformu pro jejich obranu.
main_issue: The translation fails to recognize cricket terminology, translating 'century' (100 runs) as a time period and 'century stand' literally.

src_text:
Declan didn’t even realize he was nodding until the motion was halfway done.
Something about the way Kyle spoke; calm, direct, like he actually got it. It cut through the usual static in Declan’s brain. He didn’t know what was happening, not really. Just that, for some reason, this guy already had a kind of hold on him. Not in a forced way. Just…steady. Solid. Like someone who meant what he said and wouldn’t flake the second things got hard.
No adult had ever made him feel that before. Not a teacher. Not a coach. Not even his own dad.
mt_text:
ديكلان لم يدرك حتى أنه يهز رأسه إلا عندما كانت الحركة قد بدأت بالفعل.
كان هناك شيء في طريقة كلام كايل؛ هادئ، مباشر، وكأنه يفهم حقًا. هذا الشيء اخترق الضجيج المعتاد في عقل ديكلان. لم يكن يعرف ما الذي يحدث، ليس حقًا. فقط أن هذا الشخص، لسبب ما، كان لديه نوع من السيطرة عليه. ليس بطريقة قسرية. فقط... ثابت. متين. مثل شخص يقصد ما يقوله ولن يتخلى عنك عندما تصبح الأمور صعبة.
لا أحد بالغ كان قد جعله يشعر بهذا من قبل. لا معلم. لا مدرب. ولا حتى والده.
main_issue: The translation is written in Modern Standard Arabic instead of the requested Egyptian Arabic (ar_EG) and follows English syntax too literally.

src_text: This being America, the class signifiers are slightly different from those in Britain. But in most regards, the cohort of young men hired by Elon Musk for his cost-cutting taskforce, the department of government efficiency (Doge), will be familiar to anyone who lived through the era of Boris Johnson's weapons-grade flippancy or reports of David Cameron's youthful hijinks. (Donald Trump is very flippant, of course, but his style skews locker room rather than debate chamber - or, in this case, maths olympiad.) And while politics has always run on young, volunteer energy, less common in the US, perhaps, is the imperial swagger, the sheer frivolous entitlement accompanying a crowd that has seemingly been given the keys to the kingdom.
mt_text: ई अमेरिका ह, मतलब कि वर्गीय किरदार ब्रिटेन के मुकाबले थोड़ा अलग बा। लेकिन ज्यादातर मामिला में, जवान लोग के समूह जेकरा के एलन मस्क अपने लागत-कटौती टास्कफोर्स, सरकार के कार्यक्षमता विभाग (Doge) खातिर नियुक्त कइले बा, ओहमें से केहू भी ओह समय के जिक्र कर सकेला जब बोरिस जॉनसन के हथियार-भस्म उदासीनता देखल गइल रहे, या डेविड कैमरन के युवावादी हरकत के खबर मिलत रहल। (डोनाल्ड ट्रंप बहुत उदासीन बा, बेशक, लेकिन उनकर स्टाइल लॉकर रूम से जादा बहस-कक्ष तक नइखे छूटल - या, एह मामिला में, गणित ओलंपियाड तक नइखे छूटल।) आ राजनीति में हमेशा से नवजवान, स्वयंसेवी ऊर्जा शामिल रहल बा, भले ही ई अमेरिका में कम मिलेला, शायद, लेकिन ओह लोग में साम्राज्यवादी हिम्मत, ओह भारी-भरकम बेतुका हक-मांग शामिल बा जे लागता पूरा देश के कुंजी लेके पावल बा।
main_issue: The translation relies on literal, word-for-word renderings of complex English idioms, resulting in nonsensical phrases like 'weapon-ash indifference' for 'weapons-grade flippancy'.

src_text: This feat would be more impressive if their online remarks and bios didn't flag what might diplomatically be called large gaps in their skill-sets. Musk, a man with the emotional maturity of a cartoon bank robber, is leading a group of men most of whom have no government or management experience whatsoever, let alone expertise in fields governed by the agencies they have been tasked to reform. The whole scene is reminiscent of the 90s boom in management consultancy, during which new graduates stared with frank disbelief at anyone who was over 35 and still breathing. And sure enough, as reported in the New York Times, young engineers have been overheard referring to federal employees as "dinosaurs," who have in turn called the guys in baseball caps "Muskrats."
mt_text: هذا الإنجاز كان ليصبح أكثر إبهارًا لو لم تظهر ملاحظاتهم على الإنترنت وسيرهم الذاتية بشكل واضح نقاط ضعف كبيرة في مهاراتهم. ماسك، يفتقر إلى النضج العاطفي، يقود مجموعة من الرجال أغلبهم لا يمتلكون أي خبرة في الحكومة أو الإدارة، ناهيك عن الخبرة في المجالات التي أُوكلت إليهم مهمة إصلاحها. المشهد هذا يذكرنا بالازدهار الذي حصل في مجال الاستشارات الإدارية في التسعينيات، عندما كان الخريجون الجدد يراقبون بدهشة أي شخص أكبر من 35 سنة لا يزال على قيد الحياة. والأمر الأكثر إثارة، كما ورد في صحيفة نيويورك تايمز، فقد سمع موظفون حكوميون يشيرون إلى المهندسين الشباب بـ "ديناصورات"، بينما أطلق الرجال الذين يرتدون قبعات بيسبول على الموظفين الحكوميين تسمية "مسكرات".
main_issue: The translation reverses the subject and object of the 'dinosaurs' insult and omits the 'cartoon bank robber' simile, losing the satirical tone.

src_text: Some set designs instantly make sense. Paul Wills's clever arrangement of television screens and speakers, all linked up by glowing capillary-like tubes, encapsulates Malorie Blackman's 1997 novel about a boy whose pig-heart transplant leads to a media frenzy. Andrew Exeter's lighting switches from blood red to cool blue for scenes when 13-year-old Cameron repeatedly puts himself - and, eventually, his new heart - to the test when diving at his local pool.
mt_text: Деякі декорації одразу ж стають зрозумілими. Оригінальне та продумане рішення Пола Віллса, що полягає у використанні телевізорів і динаміків, з'єднаних між собою мережею світних трубок, чудово передає суть роману Малорі Блекман 1997 року про хлопчика, якому пересадили серце свині, що викликало великий інтерес у засобах масової інформації. Освітлення, створене Ендрю Ексетером, змінюється від насиченого червоного до холодного синього кольору в сценах, де тринадцятирічний Кемерон неодноразово піддає себе – і, зрештою, своє нове серце – випробуванням, пірнаючи у місцевому басейні.
main_issue: The translation is fluent but leans toward a dry, formal register, losing the vivid imagery of 'blood red' and the intensity of 'media frenzy'.

src_text: I shouldn't be surprised - actual children are buying expensive anti-ageing potions and fretting about their nonexistent crow's feet now. But still, this dramatic normalisation of invasive beauty procedures is unnerving.
mt_text: لا ينبغي أن أشعر بالدهشة - فالأطفال الحقيقيون يشترون الآن مستحضرات مكافحة الشيخوخة باهظة الثمن ويشعرون بالقلق بشأن تجاعيد العين غير الموجودة لديهم. ولكن مع ذلك، فإن هذا التطبيع الدرامي للإجراءات التجميلية الغازية أمر مقلق.
main_issue: The translation relies on literal calques for technical and descriptive terms, particularly 'invasive' (translated as 'invading army' style) and 'dramatic'.

src_text:
Declan shifted in his seat, still trying to wrap his head around it. Living with Kyle Barnes. Having breakfast across the table from the guy who used to light it up in the league. It was a lot to take in.
Kyle let out a soft chuckle, picking up on the hesitation in Declan’s eyes. “It’s a lot to process, huh?”
Declan didn’t answer, but the look on his face said enough. He watched Kyle carefully, the weight of it all pressing in.
Kyle leaned back in his chair, settling into a more relaxed posture, but his eyes stayed sharp and knowing. “I’ve been where you’re sitting. New town. New team. Big pressure. It can mess with your head if you let it.”
mt_text:
Declan se pomaknuo u sjedištu i još je pokušavao obuhvatiti situaciju. Staniti s Kyleom Barnesom. Zajednički doručak s tipom koji je u ligi bio pravi pušač. Bilo je previše da bi se to moglo probaviti.
Kyle je izdahnuo lagano i nasmijao se, primijetivši dvojbu u Declanovim očima. „Teško je sve to probaviti, zar ne?”
Declan nije odgovorio, ali izgled na njegovom licu govorio je dovoljno. Pozorno je promatrao Kylea, a sve to je na njega vršilo pritisak.
Kyle se naslonio natrag u stolicu i opustio se, ali oči su mu ostale blage i pune znanja. „Bio sam na tvojom mjestu. Novo mjesto. Nova ekipa. Veliko je pritisak. Ako dopustiš, može ti zapetljati glavu.”
main_issue: The translation contains a catastrophic mistranslation of 'light it up' as 'smoker' and features non-existent words and grammatical errors.

🧵 Індіго вирушила назустріч новій долі!
Зверніть увагу: усі важливі оновлення починатимуться із емодзі нитки.
Я намагаюся переїхати до Німеччини та почати нове життя! Слідкуйте за цією сторінкою, щоб відстежувати хід подій.
Останні 32 години були жахливими! Панічні атаки, крайня втома, нудота.
Але подивіться, де я зараз! На аеродромі!!! Я це зробив!
Я занадто старалась і, зрештою, не спала 32 години. Наразі (коли я пишу це) я перебуваю на 33-й годині. Озираючись назад, можна сказати, що я вибрала правильний шлях. Неможливо було зробити іншим чином. Поза всяким сумнівом, СДУГ – це тварина, яку потрібно приборкати.
EDIT: тасманійський.
За останні 3 місяці я багато досягла! Я думала, що зможу це зробити за 1 місяць, але сильно недооцінила УСІХ аспектів. Були випадки, коли мені двічі доводилося перебронювати політ, але зараз у мене два терміни, і цей літак — остання можливість!
Ого… Я зустрілася з ріелтором, прибрала більшу частину будинку, віддала третину своїх речей, відповідним людям дали доступ до решти, а моя сестра може бути уповноваженою особою, якщо знадобиться. Знаю, що майже сім документів легалізовано апостилем, мої дипломні звання визнані в Німеччині, курс навчання іноземною мовою заброньовано, апартаменти (можливо, шахрайство) заброньовано, основні документи оформлені. Також маю міжнародний сертифікат, який доводить, що я не злочинець! Настав час діяти!
main_issue: The translation features inconsistent gender agreement for the narrator and uses overly formal or literal phrasing that misses the casual, frantic tone of the source.

src_text:
Declan hesitated only for a second before sliding into his seat next to Ryan, who was already digging in like it was a competitive sport. He glanced at his own portion and, after a quick taste, instantly understood why. The chicken parm was insanely good.
Declan took another bite, savoring the perfect balance of crispy breading and rich marinara. The flavors were way better than he expected, so much so that he couldn't help but mutter, “This is fucking good.”
The second the words left his mouth, the room fell dead silent. Ryan’s fork froze halfway to his mouth, his eyes snapping up to Declan with a mixture of shock and disbelief, like Declan had just unleashed a bomb at the dinner table.
mt_text:
Deklan váhal jen na vteřinu, než si sedl vedle Ryana, který se do jídla pustil, jako by šlo o soutěžní sport. Podíval se na svou porci a po rychlé ochutnávce okamžitě pochopil proč. Kuřecí parmazán byl neskutečně dobrý.
Declan si dal další sousto a vychutnal si dokonalou rovnováhu křupavého panáčku a bohaté marinády. Chutě byly o dost lepší, než čekal, natolik, že si nemohl pomoci a zamumlal: „To je kurva dobrý.“
V okamžiku, kdy ta slova opustila jeho ústa, ve místnosti nastalo hrobové ticho. Ryanova vidlička zamrzla v polovině cesty k ústům, jeho oči se upřely na Declana se směsicí šoku a nedůvěry, jako by Declan právě u večeře odpálil bombu.
main_issue: The translation contains a critical nonsensical error ('panáčku' for breading), a basic grammatical error ('ve místnosti'), and inconsistent spelling of the protagonist's name.

src_text: The fact there is no story here makes Meghan's insistence that she is getting in front of the story all the more enjoyable, but the more curious aspect of the announcement is the "you" to whom she refers and the idea she clearly has of her audience. The celebrity lifestyle space is crowded, ferocious and notoriously fickle, littered with the carcasses of former brand leaders such as Chrissy Teigen. Target and Bloomingdales distanced themselves from the star around the same time as she was accused of, and later apologised for, cyberbullying a woman called Courtney. The space is also dominated by market leaders such as Gwyneth Paltrow and Rachel Zoe.

Declan didn’t even realize he was nodding until the motion was halfway done.
Something about the way Kyle spoke; calm, direct, like he actually got it. It cut through the usual static in Declan’s brain. He didn’t know what was happening, not really. src_text:
“Yeah, I think so,” Ryan replied, his voice steady despite the nervous flutter in his stomach. There was a determination in his eyes that reassured Kyle.
“Hey, you’ll do great,” Kyle said, offering a comforting smile. “But before you head to class, Mom wants a quick picture.”
Ryan groaned, anticipating the familiar routine. “Do we have to—”
“Ryan,” Kyle replied, his tone light but firm. “Less attitude, please. You know the drill.”
They stepped out of the car, and Kyle quickly snapped a picture he knew Nicole would cherish. It wasn’t Ryan’s most enthusiastic smile, but it was a smile nonetheless.

«Так, я думаю, що так», — відповів Райан, його голос був стійким, незважаючи на нервове тремтіння у животі. У його очах була рішучість, яка заспокоїла Кайла.
«Ей, ти впораєшся добре», — сказав Кайл, даруючи заспокійливу посмішку. — «Але перед тим, як піти на урок, мама хоче швидке фото».
Райан застогнав, очікуючи знайому рутину. «Чи мусимо ми…?»
«Райане», — відповів Кайл, його тон був легким, але твердим. — «Менше агресії, будь ласка. Ти знаєш правила».
Вони вийшли з машини, і Кайл швидко зробив фото, яке, він знав, Ніколь оцінить. Це не була найентузіастичніша посмішка Райана, але це була посмішка.
main_issue: The translation contains several literal calques and a significant register/meaning error where 'attitude' is translated as 'aggression'.

डेक्लन के पता भी ना चलल कि ऊ सिर हिलावत बा जब तक कि आधा काम हो ना गइल।
काइल के बोले के तरीका में कुछ बात रहे; शांत, सीधा, जइसे ऊ सच में समझत होखे। ई डेक्लन के दिमाग में के आम शोर के काट दिहलस। ओकरा पता ना रहे कि का होखत बा, सच में ना। बस अतना कि, कवनो कारण से, एह आदमी के पहिलहीं से ओकरा पर एक तरह के पकड़ रहे। जबरदस्ती वाला ना। बस… स्थिर। ठोस। जइसे केहू जवन कहत होखे उहे करत होखे आउर मुश्किल आवते भाग ना जाई।
कवनो बड़ आदमी ओकरा के पहिले अइसन महसूस ना करवले रहे। ना कवनो शिक्षक। ना कवनो कोच। हियाँ तक कि ओकर आपन बाप भी ना।
main_issue: The translation relies on literal renderings of English phrases like 'halfway done' and 'static', which sound unnatural in Bhojpuri.

src_text: Blackman's novel is itself a deep dive: this "what would you do?" book for young readers considers ethics, animal rights, othering and empathy. Keeping the pre-social media setting, Winsome Pinnock's new adaptation draws upon the heightened poetic style of Blackman's opening chapter. Pinnock retains the narrator's spirited interest in wordplay, boosts the wisdom of Cameron's Nan and makes water more of a unifying theme throughout the story. Her version also takes a thrilling new turn towards the end.
mt_text: Роман Блекмена сам по собі є глибоким дослідженням: ця книга для молодих читачів «що б ви зробили?» розглядає етику, права тварин, відчуження та емпатію. У новій адаптації Вінсома Пінокка, що зберігає передсоціальне середовище, використано піднесений поетичний стиль першого розділу Блекмена. Пінок зберігає живий інтерес оповідача до гри словами, підсилює мудрість Камеронської бабусі та робить воду більш об'єднуючим мотивом на всьому протязі історії. Її версія також робить захоплюючий новий поворот до кінця.
main_issue: The translation misgenders female authors (Blackman, Pinnock) and uses the common 'на протязі' calque instead of 'протягом' or 'упродовж'.

src_text:
Declan shook his hand, barely keeping the awe off his face. A breath slipped out, half disbelief, half nerves, and before he could stop himself, his mouth got ahead of his brain.
“I had you on my fantasy team last year. Not gonna lie, you lost me the championship.”
Declan pressed his lips together the second the words left his mouth, the grin fading as panic crept in. Nice one, he thought. Way to chirp an NHL vet five minutes into meeting him. He resisted the urge to bury his face in his hands.
A snicker sounded behind him. Declan glanced over his shoulder to see Coach Luc with a hand half-covering his mouth, doing a poor job of hiding his amusement.
mt_text:
Deklan mu potřásl rukou a sotva skrýval úžas. Výdech mu unikl, napůl z nevěřícnosti, napůl z nervozity, a než se mohl zastavit, jeho ústa předběhla mozek.
Měl jsem tě loni ve svém fantasy týmu. Nebudu lhát, kvůli tobě jsem prohrál šampionát.
Declan stiskl rty ve chvíli, kdy mu slova vyšla z úst, úsměv pohasínal, jak ho začala přepadat panika. Výborně, pomyslel si. Super nápad říct tohle hokejovému veteránovi z NHL pět minut poté, co ho potkal. Odolával nutkání schovat si obličej do dlaní.
Za ním se ozval potichu smích. Declan se ohlédl přes rameno a uviděl trenéra Luca, jak si rukou zakrývá ústa a marně se snaží skrýt své pobavení.
main_issue: The translation relies on literal calques of English idioms and suffers from a major character name inconsistency (Deklan vs. Declan).

src_text:
Declan didn’t even realize he was nodding until the motion was halfway done.
Something about the way Kyle spoke; calm, direct, like he actually got it. It cut through the usual static in Declan’s brain. He didn’t know what was happening, not really. Just that, for some reason, this guy already had a kind of hold on him. Not in a forced way. Just…steady. Solid. Like someone who meant what he said and wouldn’t flake the second things got hard.
No adult had ever made him feel that before. Not a teacher. Not a coach. Not even his own dad.
mt_text:
Деклан није ни приметио да је кимнуо док већ није био на пола тог покрета.
Неко у начину на који је Кајл говорио – мирно, директно, као да је заиста разумео – пробило је кроз уобичајену „буку“ у Деклановом мозгу. Он није знао шта се дешава, заправо. Само је знао да, из неког разлога, овај човек већ има неку врсту утицаја на њега. Не на неки наметљив начин. Само… стабилно. Поуздано. Као неко ко је искрен у својим речима и ко се неће предомислити чим ствари постану тешке.
Ниједан одрасли никада га није учинио да се тако осећа. Ни учитељ. Ни тренер. Чак ни његов отац.
main_issue: The translation relies on heavy syntactic calques from English, particularly the 'made him feel' structure and a semantic error using 'Someone' instead of 'Something'.

src_text:
Declan shifted in his seat, still trying to wrap his head around it. Living with Kyle Barnes. Having breakfast across the table from the guy who used to light it up in the league. It was a lot to take in.
Kyle let out a soft chuckle, picking up on the hesitation in Declan’s eyes. “It’s a lot to process, huh?”
Declan didn’t answer, but the look on his face said enough. He watched Kyle carefully, the weight of it all pressing in.
Kyle leaned back in his chair, settling into a more relaxed posture, but his eyes stayed sharp and knowing. “I’ve been where you’re sitting. New town. New team. Big pressure. It can mess with your head if you let it.”
mt_text:
Деклан переменил положение в кресле, всё ещё пытаясь осознать это. Жить с Кайлом Барнсом. Завтракать за одним столом с парнем, который когда-то блистал в лиге. Это было сложно осознать.
Кайл тихо рассмеялся, заметив нерешительность в глазах Деклана. «Это многое, чтобы осознать, правда?»
Деклан не ответил, но выражение его лица говорило само за себя. Он внимательно смотрел на Кайла, чувствуя, как всё это давит на него.
Кайл откинулся на спинку стула, приняв более расслабленную позу, но его глаза оставались острыми и проницательными. «Я был на твоём месте. Новый город. Новая команда. Большое давление. Это может выбить тебя из колеи, если ты позволишь этому случиться».
main_issue: The translation relies on repetitive vocabulary and a clunky, literal rendering of 'It’s a lot to process' that sounds unnatural in Russian.

src_text: Game 18 of #UniversityChallenge was Durham vs Open University. I was rooting for Durham, who have a great team, but in the end to my surprise Open took it, after a number of rather slow answers. I was glad Durham got Marcel Duchamp right on one question; too bad no one knew Yoko Ono as a Fluxus artist. Much respect for Durham Nash, especially on bird silhouettes and flags.
mt_text: 18. kolo #UniversityChallenge bilo je Durham protiv Otvoreni univerzitet. Ja sam se zalagao za Durham, koji imaju odličnu ekipu, ali na kraju, u iznenađenju, Otvoreni univerzitet je pobedio, posle nekoliko vrlo sporih odgovora. Bilo mi je drago što je Durham uspeo da Marcela Duchampa dobro odgovori na jednoj pitanju; žalosno, niko nije znao da je Yoko Ono Fluxus umetnik. Veliko poštovanje za Durham Nash, posebno na siluetama ptica i zastavama.
main_issue: The translation contains several literal calques of English idioms and basic grammatical errors in gender and case agreement.

src_text: European politicians more rational and less self-serving than Johnson are trying to formulate the correct response to Trump's rapid and reckless redrawing of the postwar world disorder in his own, and Russia's, interests. The correct response is to shit your pants. On Tuesday, Trump even blamed Ukraine itself for being invaded, which is a bit like blaming E Jean Carroll herself for being sexually abused in a department store changing room. Couldn't she have cut a pre-emptive deal before things escalated? Victims! Always blaming someone else. But Trump has put the idea that the invasion of Ukraine is Ukraine's fault out there now, on the world stage, amplified by his collaborators in the tech bro media, and it will gradually calcify into one of those persistent alternative facts. By Wednesday he'd called Zelenskyy a dictator (and a mediocre comedian, which in my opinion is even worse).
mt_text: Европски политичари, рационалнији и мање сервилни од Џонсона, покушавају да формулишу прави одговор на брзо и непрепазљиво преисцртавање светског поретка после рата од стране Трампа, у складу са његовим и Русијом. Прави одговор је да изгубите самоконтролу. У уторак је Трамп чак оптужио Украјину за то што је била нападнута, што је поприлично слично оптуживању саме Е Џејн Карол да је била сексуално злостављана у пробном кабини продавнице одеће. Не би ли могла да закључи претходни споразум пре него што су ствари ескалирале? Жртве! Увек неког оптужују. Али Трамп је сада изнео идеју да је Украјина крива за инвазију на Украјину, на светској сцени, уз појачавање од стране његових сарадника у технолошком сектору медија, и постепено ће се то претворити у један од оних упорних алтернативних чињеница. До среде је назвао Зеленског диктатором (и усредњег комичара, што је, по мом мишљењу, још горе).
main_issue: Mistranslation of 'self-serving' as 'servile' and multiple grammatical errors in noun declension and gender agreement.

src_text: I learned about salmon sperm in a Sunday Times article on the "skincare secrets" of "26 tastemakers," which I read, increasingly aghast. It was a litany of lasers, microneedling, injectables and proprietary treatments with silly names that left me shouting crossly at my laptop: "But what does it do?" The only treatment I related to at all was Marina Abramović recounting how a friend of her mother's put hot mashed potato on her face to temporarily erase wrinkles; Abramović herself uses "thermage radiofrequency." No wonder a salmon sperm practitioner says it's "an exciting time in the aesthetic industry."
mt_text: サンデー・タイムズ紙に載っていた「26人のテイストメーカー」の「スキンケアの秘密」という記事で、私はますます驚きながらサーモンの精子について学んだ。レーザー、マイクロニードル、注射剤、そして馬鹿げた名前の独自治療法が並んでいて、私はラップトップに向かって「でも、何の効果があるの？」と怒鳴りつけた。私が少しでも共感できたのは、マリナ・アブラモヴィッチが、母親の友人が彼女の顔にホットマッシュポテトを塗って一時的にしわを消した話を語ったことだけだった。アブラモヴィッチ自身は「サーマージーラジオ波」を使っている。サーモンの精子を使う美容師が「エステ業界は今、エキサイティングな時期」と言っているのも不思議ではない。
main_issue: The translation uses '美容師' (hairdresser) for a medical/aesthetic practitioner and employs literal phrasing like 'ますます驚きながら' which misses the cynical tone of 'aghast'.

Зар није могла да склопи превентивни договор пре него што су ствари ескалирале? Жртве! Увек окривљују неког другог. Али Трамп је сада ставио идеју да је инвазија на Украјину кривица Украјине, на светску сцену, појачану од стране његових сарадника у технолошким медијима, и она ће се постепено претворити у једну од оних упорних алтернативних чињеница. src_text: Some set designs instantly make sense. Paul Wills's clever arrangement of television screens and speakers, all linked up by glowing capillary-like tubes, encapsulates Malorie Blackman's 1997 novel about a boy whose pig-heart transplant leads to a media frenzy. Andrew Exeter's lighting switches from blood red to cool blue for scenes when 13-year-old Cameron repeatedly puts himself - and, eventually, his new heart - to the test when diving at his local pool.
mt_text: Деякі сценічні рішення одразу стають зрозумілими. Продумана композиція Пола Віллса з телевізійними екранами та динаміками, з’єднаними світловими трубками, що нагадують капіляри, втілює у собі суть роману Малорі Блекмен 1997 року про хлопчика, якому пересадили серце свині, що викликало ажіотаж у засобах масової інформації. Освітлення Ендрю Ексетера змінюється від криваво-червоного до прохолодно-блакитного у сценах, де 13-річний Кемерон неодноразово випробовує себе — а згодом і своє нове серце — на міцність під час стрибків у місцевому басейні.
main_issue: The translation is accurate but occasionally uses wordy or slightly formal collocations where punchier, more natural phrasing would better suit a review.

Дејклан није ни приметио да кима док није већ напола кимао.

main_issue: The translation relies heavily on English syntax, especially in the final paragraph, resulting in ungrammatical structures and unnatural phrasing.

src_text: While Guinness may be at significant risk of losing its edge among, say, people who queue outside bakeries, this hardly matters to its bottom line. It has surpassed cult status, and has in essence become too big to fail. Debra Crew, chief executive of Diageo, the drinks conglomerate that owns the brand, told press this week that demand for the stout in October and November 2024 had surpassed even the amount called for during St Patrick's Day celebrations, noting that the clamour was "unprecedented." Crew also stated that Diageo is spending €200m (£170m) on a new Guinness factory in Kildare to keep supplies up to the level now required.
mt_text: في حين أن جينيس قد تكون في خطر كبير من فقدان مكانتها المرموقة بين، على سبيل المثال، الأشخاص الذين ينتظرون في الصف أمام المخابز، فإن ذلك لا يهم كثيراً بالنسبة لمبيعاتها النهائية. فقد تجاوزت العلامة التجارية مكانة الأسطورة، وأصبحت في حقيقة الأمر كبيرة جداً بحيث لا يمكن تدميرها. صرحت دبّور كرو، الرئيسة التنفيذية لشركة دياجيو، وهي مجموعة شركات المشروبات المالكة للعلامة التجارية، للصحافة هذا الأسبوع أن الطلب على ارقطان جينيس في أكتوبر ونوفمبر 2024 تجاوز حتى الكمية المطلوبة خلال احتفالات يوم القديس باتريك، مشيرةً إلى أن الضجة كانت "غير مسبوقة". كما ذكرت كرو أن دياجيو تنفق 200 مليون يورو (170 مليون جنيه إسترليني) على إنشاء مصنع جينيس جديد في كيلدار للحفاظ على إمدادات ترقى إلى المستوى المطلوب حالياً.
main_issue: Critical transliteration error (Debra as 'Wasp') and the use of a nonsensical/hallucinated word 'ارقطان' for stout.

src_text: I think a lot of the other stuff is such a flagrant violation. These 40 cases.... It's like an advent calendar of illegality, Greg. Every day you turn up another flap and another constitutional provision statute or regulation is violated, started on day one. Many of these are dead losers for Trump, except with complicit, corrupt, conflicted Thomas and Alito.
mt_text: Мислим да већина других ствари такође представља очигледно кршење закона. Ових 40 случајева… То је као адвентски календар незаконитости, Греге – сваког дана отвориш нови „прозорчић“ и видиш још једно кршење уставне одредбе, закона или прописа, почевши од првог дана. Већина ових случајева је за Трампа унапред изгубљена, осим ако не добије помоћ од саучеснички настројених, корумпираних или у сукобу интереса судија попут Томаса и Алита.
main_issue: The translation is slightly too wordy and formal, losing the punchy rhetorical style of the original adjectives.

src_text: Blackman's novel is itself a deep dive: this "what would you do?" book for young readers considers ethics, animal rights, othering and empathy. Keeping the pre-social media setting, Winsome Pinnock's new adaptation draws upon the heightened poetic style of Blackman's opening chapter. Pinnock retains the narrator's spirited interest in wordplay, boosts the wisdom of Cameron's Nan and makes water more of a unifying theme throughout the story. Her version also takes a thrilling new turn towards the end.
mt_text: Сам роман Блекман – це глибоке занурення: ця книга для юних читачів у жанрі "що б ти зробив?" розглядає питання етики, прав тварин, відчуження та співчуття. Зберігаючи досоціальномережеву обстановку, нова адаптація Вінсом Піннок використовує підвищений поетичний стиль першого розділу книги Блекман.
