Нешто у начину на који је Кајл говорио; смирено, директно, као да стварно разуме. То је пресекло уобичајени шум у Деклановој глави. Није знао шта се дешава, заправо. Само да, из неког разлога, овај момак је већ имао неку врсту утицаја на њега. Не на силу. Већ… сталожено. Чврсто. Као неко ко мисли оно што каже и неће нестати чим ствари постану тешке.

src_text: It's ridiculous, of course, as is the hello-little-people tone adopted by the former royal highness, in keeping with the general attitude of the famous towards the unfamous - namely, one of stating the bleeding obvious as though sharing the key to existence. Still, I find myself sympathising with Meghan, who in her Instagram post and its accompanying video faces the stark challenge of having to pad out thin material. "This is what I do," she says to camera, "and I haven't been able to share it with you for the past few years, but now I can." What is the "it" here, and what has stopped her from sharing it? (It may involve jam, as did her previous brand, which she launched by sending "limited edition" jars of preserve to, among others, Kris Jenner, Mindy Kaling and Tracee Ellis Ross, who apparently also got a fresh bag of lemons.) Anyway, whatever the content, she hints darkly at some "whispers" abroad that forced her to jump the gun and share news of As Ever before she was ready - because "I wanted you to hear it from me first."
mt_text: 这当然荒谬至极，正如这位前王室成员居高临下的亲民口吻——名流对待普通人的典型做派，总把不言自明的道理说得像在揭示生命真谛。不过我倒对梅根生出几分同情，她在帖文和视频中直面着内容空洞的窘境。"这就是我的生活，"她对镜头说道，"过去几年一直没能与你们分享，但现在可以了。"这个"它"究竟指什么？又是什么阻碍了她分享？（或许与果酱有关，就像她上次推出品牌时，给克里斯·詹纳、敏迪·卡灵等名人寄送"限量版"果酱罐，附赠新鲜柠檬一袋。）总之，她神秘暗示某些"流言蜚语"迫使她仓促提前宣布"如初"——因为"希望你们首先从我这里听到"。
main_issue: The translation captures the literal meaning but softens the sharp, sarcastic British register of the original (e.g., 'bleeding obvious').

src_text:
Declan hesitated only for a second before sliding into his seat next to Ryan, who was already digging in like it was a competitive sport. He glanced at his own portion and, after a quick taste, instantly understood why. The chicken parm was insanely good.
Declan took another bite, savoring the perfect balance of crispy breading and rich marinara. The flavors were way better than he expected, so much so that he couldn't help but mutter, “This is fucking good.”
The second the words left his mouth, the room fell dead silent. Ryan’s fork froze halfway to his mouth, his eyes snapping up to Declan with a mixture of shock and disbelief, like Declan had just unleashed a bomb at the dinner table.
mt_text:
Declan hesiteeris vaid hetke, enne kui libises oma kohale Rjani kõrvale, kes juba innukalt sööma asendas. Ta vaatas oma osa üle ja pärast kiiret maitsetõmmet mõistis kohe, miks. Kana parmesan oli lihtsalt hullult hea.
Declan pistis veel suutäie, nautides krõbeda paneeringu ja rikkaliku tomatimahlakastme täiuslikku tasakaalu. Maitsed olid palju paremad, kui ta ootas, nii palju, et ta ei suutnud end tagasi hoida ja mumis: „See on kuradi hea.“
Niipea kui sõnad suust välja tulid, ruum vaikis. Rjani kahvel külmus pooleli teel suhu, tema silmad hüppasid Declanile vastu šoki, uskumatus ja peaaegu kohmakusega, nagu oleks Declan just lõhkepommi õhtalauale visanud.
main_issue: The translation contains several lexical errors (asendas vs asus), typos (Rjani, õhtalauale), and unnatural calques of English idioms.

src_text: In these days of darkness we must take delight where we can find it, and one reliable source is the Duchess of Sussex. In her latest venture, Meghan, who occupies the tricky position of having a large platform and not enough to fill it (see also: Brooklyn Beckham), launched a new lifestyle brand that, she promised in a short video, would be "beautifully weaving together everything I cherish - food, gardening, entertaining, thoughtful living and finding joy in the everyday." The brand American Riviera Orchard is dead. Long live the new brand, As Ever.
mt_text: V těchto temných dnech se musíme radovat, kdekoli to jde, a jedním spolehlivým zdrojem je vévodkyně ze Sussexu. Ve svém nejnovějším počinu Meghan, která zaujímá obtížnou pozici, kdy má velkou platformu, ale ne dostatek obsahu, aby ji zaplnila (viz také Brooklyn Beckham), spustila novou lifestylovou značku, která, jak slíbila ve krátkém videu, bude „krásně propojovat vše, co miluji – jídlo, zahradničení, zábavu, promyšlený život a nacházení radosti v každodenním životě.“ Značka American Riviera Orchard je mrtvá. Ať žije nová značka, As Ever.
main_issue: The translation contains a basic grammatical error in preposition vocalization and relies on literal calques for lifestyle terminology.

Declan shifted in his seat, still trying to wrap his head around it. Living with Kyle Barnes. Having breakfast across the table from the guy who used to light it up in the league. It was a lot to take in.
Kyle let out a soft chuckle, picking up on the hesitation in Declan’s eyes. “It’s a lot to process, huh?”
Declan didn’t answer, but the look on his face said enough. He watched Kyle carefully, the weight of it all pressing in.

Declan se pohnul na svém sedadle a stále se snažil přijít na to, co to znamená. Bydlet s Kylem Barnesem. Jíst snídani naproti chlapovi, který býval v lize tím nejlepším hráčem. Bylo toho na něj moc.

src_text:
“Yeah, I think so,” Ryan replied, his voice steady despite the nervous flutter in his stomach. There was a determination in his eyes that reassured Kyle.
“Hey, you’ll do great,” Kyle said, offering a comforting smile. “But before you head to class, Mom wants a quick picture.”
Ryan groaned, anticipating the familiar routine. “Do we have to—”

— Так, думаю, що так, — відповів Райан, його голос був стійким, попри нервове тремтіння в животі. У його очах була рішучість, яка заспокоїла Кайла.
— Гей, ти впораєшся, — сказав Кайл, даруючи йому заспокійливу посмішку. — Але перш ніж ти підеш на заняття, мама хоче зробити швидке фото.
Райан застогнав, передбачаючи знайому рутину. — Чи обов'язково…
— Райан, — відповів Кайл, його тон був легким, але твердим. — Менше нахабства, будь ласка. Ти знаєш, що робити.
Вони вийшли з машини, і Кайл швидко зробив знімок, який він знав, що Ніколь буде зберігати. Це не була найщиріша посмішка Райана, але все ж це була посмішка.
main_issue: The translation relies on heavy syntactic calques and literal renderings of English idioms, particularly in the final paragraph.

src_text: I've always thought that one of the most insoluble aesthetic problems going is remaking a movie masterpiece. I certainly understand the impulse to passionately re-engage such a work, but if the definition of a masterwork is something peerless at what it sought to accomplish, how do you remake it without simply reiterating it? There's a reason no one has tried second versions of Fellini's "8½" or Coppola's "The Godfather" or Polanski's "Chinatown." In the case of Fellini's achievement, is someone going to produce a more harrowing portrait of the self-deluding toxicity of male narcissism? In the case of Coppola's, of the corrosive effects of power? Or in the case of Polanski's, of the Hey-nothing-personal malevolence of late-model capitalism? (Water itself in that movie turns out to be the commodity that's manipulated for profit.)
mt_text: Я завжди вважав, що однією з найбільш невирішених естетичних проблем є переробка майстерного фільму. Я безумовно розумію бажання знову пристрасно поринути у такий твір, але, якщо визначення шедевра полягає в тому, що це вища, неперевершена форма досягнення поставлених цілей, то як його переробити, не повторюючи при цьому сам фільм? Є причина, чому ніхто досі не спробував творити другі версії фільмів Фелліні "8¾", або Копполи "Хрещений батько", або Поланскі "Китайський квартал". У випадку з досягненнями Фелліні, чи спробує хтось зробити більш жахливий портрет самообманної токсичності чоловічого нарцисизму? А в випадку з досягненнями Копполи – більш гострий образ руйнівного впливу влади? Або, у випадку з досягненнями Поланскі, більш гострий образ розлюченої, нічим не виправданої злостивості сучасного капіталізму? (Сама вода в цьому фільмі перетворюється на ресурс, яким маніпулюють заради наживи.)
main_issue: The translation contains a factual error in a famous movie title (8¾ instead of 8½) and fails to capture the cynical 'nothing personal' tone, rendering it as 'furious'.

src_text: I'm pretty sure that what will finally usher the Guinness hype train back into the station will be the same thing that came for Aperol spritzes and truffle: overexposure. The Aperol, once a genuinely trendy, vaguely Sex and the City-ish aperitivo-style drink is now a cultural behemoth in its own right - delicious, certainly, but definitely no longer "cool." And truffle, well ... would you like a side of truffle fries for that truffle mac and cheese garnished with truffle oil?
mt_text: Naitumokie enkitok eng’eno nabo olo “Guinness hype train” aisho oloshoo, nabo olo erishata Aperol spritzes na truffle: overexposure. Aperol, nabo oshomo trendy, oleng “Sex and the City”-ish aperitivo-style drink, sasa ni jumba la utamaduni — lazima ni tamu, lakini si “cool” tena. Na truffle... hmm... kama nakipai naita kama truffle fries, truffle mac na cheese iliyojaa na mafuta ya truffle?
main_issue: The translation is a heavy mix of Swahili and English (code-switching) rather than a proper Maasai translation, failing to use Maasai grammar or vocabulary for key segments.
